name: gast

metadata.name: gast
metadata.summary: Python AST that abstracts the underlying Python version

documentation:
A generic AST to represent Python2 and Python3's Abstract Syntax Tree(AST).

GAST provides a compatibility layer between the AST of various Python versions,
as produced by `ast.parse` from the standard `ast` module.

functions.name: gast.gast.copy_location
documentation:
Copy source location (`lineno`, `col_offset`, `end_lineno`, and
`end_col_offset` attributes) from *old_node* to *new_node* if possible,
and return *new_node*.
the following are directly imported from python3.8's Lib/ast.py  #

functions.name: gast.gast.fix_missing_locations
documentation:
When you compile a node tree with compile(), the compiler expects lineno
and col_offset attributes for every node that supports them.  This is
rather tedious to fill in for generated nodes, so this helper adds these
attributes recursively where not already set, by setting them to the values
of the parent node.  It works recursively starting at *node*.

functions.name: gast.gast.increment_lineno
documentation:
Increment the line number and end line number of each node in the tree
starting at *node* by *n*. This is useful to "move code" to a different
location in a file.

methods.name: gast.ast2.Ast2ToGAst.visit_Module

classes.name: gast.unparser._Precedence
documentation: Precedence table that originated from python grammar.

classes.name: gast.unparser._Unparser

methods.name: gast.unparser._Unparser.interleave
documentation: Call f on each item in seq, calling inter() in between.

methods.name: gast.unparser._Unparser.items_view
documentation:
Traverse and separate the given *items* with a comma and append it to
the buffer. If *items* is a single item sequence, a trailing comma
will be added.

methods.name: gast.unparser._Unparser.maybe_newline
documentation: Adds a newline if it isn't the start of generated source

methods.name: gast.unparser._Unparser.fill
documentation:
Indent a piece of text and append it, according to the current
indentation level

methods.name: gast.unparser._Unparser.write
documentation: Append a piece of text

methods.name: gast.unparser._Unparser.block
documentation:
A context manager for preparing the source for blocks. It adds
the character':', increases the indentation on enter and decreases
the indentation on exit. If *extra* is given, it will be directly
appended after the colon character.

methods.name: gast.unparser._Unparser.delimit
documentation:
A context manager for preparing the source for expressions. It adds
*start* to the buffer and enters, after exit it adds *end*.

methods.name: gast.unparser._Unparser.require_parens
documentation: Shortcut to adding precedence related parens

methods.name: gast.unparser._Unparser.get_raw_docstring
documentation:
If a docstring node is found in the body of the *node* parameter,
return that docstring node, None otherwise.

Logic mirrored from `_PyAST_GetDocString`.

methods.name: gast.unparser._Unparser.visit
documentation:
Outputs a source code string that, if converted back to an ast
(using ast.parse) will generate an AST equivalent to *node*
Note: as visit() resets the output text, do NOT rely on
NodeVisitor.generic_visit to handle any nodes (as it calls back in to
the subclass visit() method, which resets self._source to an empty list)

methods.name: gast.unparser._Unparser._str_literal_helper
documentation:
Helper for writing string literals, minimizing escapes.
Returns the tuple (string literal to write, possible quote types).

methods.name: gast.unparser._Unparser._write_str_avoiding_backslashes
documentation: Write string literal value with a best effort attempt to avoid backslashes.

Methods in this class recursively traverse an AST and
output source code for the abstract syntax; original formatting
is disregarded.
